title: Calcitrapa adami
intro: Calcitrapa adami, also known as Adam's Calamint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and provide antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Calcitrapa adami include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calcitrapa adami include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
taxonomy: Calcitrapa adami is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family is part of the larger order Brassicales. The order Brassicales is further classified within the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subclass of the larger division Magnoliophyta. Magnoliophyta is a division of flowering plants, and Calcitrapa adami is an example of a plant within this division. The kingdom to which Calcitrapa adami belongs is Plantae.
